title: Bigelowia nudata
taxonomy: Bigelowia nudata is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is native to North America and is commonly known as the rough-haired bigelowia. The plant is a perennial herb that grows up to a certain height and has a distinctive hairy appearance. Bigelowia nudata is classified in the order Asterales and the class Magnoliopsida. It is also known by its scientific name, which is a unique identifier for the species within the plant kingdom.
uses: Bigelowia nudata, also known as Bigelow's goldenaster, is a plant species with reported medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used in folk medicine to treat various ailments. intro: Bigelowia nudata, also known as naked bigelowia, is a perennial wildflower native to North America. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiviral, and antibacterial properties. The bioactive constituents of Bigelowia nudata include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Bigelowia nudata is often used to make herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly consumed for their purported health benefits.
